_comment: EXAMPLE Worker Pool Configuration for the multi-worker tutorial.
_how_to_use: To try the worker pool, copy this file to config/worker_config.json (back up the existing one first), then restart the server. The shipped config/worker_config.json has the pool DISABLED by default so the platform runs single-process out of the box.

worker_pool._enabled_note: true = run DAGs across separate OS processes (true parallelism). false = single-process mode.
worker_pool._num_workers_note: Number of worker processes to spawn. Use an integer, or the string 'auto' to use the machine's CPU count. Start with the number of physical cores.
worker_pool._stabilization_note: Pause after workers report ready before dispatching DAGs, so module imports finish first.
worker_pool._restart_note: If a worker process crashes, the pool restarts it (up to max_restart_attempts) and re-dispatches its DAGs.

affinity._strategy_note: How DAGs without an explicit worker_affinity are placed. weight_based packs by estimated CPU cost; least_loaded picks the emptiest worker; round_robin cycles; random scatters.
affinity._rebalance_note: Periodically move DAGs between workers if load is more than rebalance_threshold (30%) out of balance. Pinned/exclusive DAGs are never moved.
affinity._permission_note: Master switches for the per-DAG worker_affinity options. If false, those options are ignored.

communication._lmdb_note: DAGs on different worker processes cannot share an in-memory queue (separate memory spaces). Cross-worker pub/sub uses a memory-mapped LMDB store instead. Use lmdb:// sources/destinations to pass data between DAGs on different workers.

defaults._defaults_note: Used by weight_based placement when a DAG does not declare its own weight/priority. Heavier DAGs get spread out; higher-priority DAGs are placed first.
